title: barleria courtallica
intro: Barleria courtallica, also known as the Indian bugloss, is a flowering plant that belongs to the family Acanthaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Barleria courtallica include its ability to treat skin conditions such as eczema, acne, and wounds, as well as its potential to reduce fever and relieve respiratory issues. In herbal preparations, the leaves and flowers of Barleria courtallica are typically used to make infusions, decoctions, and ointments that can be applied topically or consumed internally.
